channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Flag plant, Gus Johnson, and fake tough guys -- 16 thoughts on Michigan football win over Ohio State
item.description: From a flag plant to Gus Johnson to fake tough guys -- here are 16 thoughts on Michigan football's fourth-straight win over Ohio State.
item.dc:creator: Derick Hutchinson, Local 4

item.title: ESPN's Kirk Herbstreit demands stiffer penalties for players involved in flag fights
item.description: ESPN college football analyst Kirk Herbstreit called for steeper consequences for those involved in fights over flag-planting incidents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kurtenbach: The music has stopped playing for the ‘last dance’ 49ers
item.description: The Niners have been outscored 73-20 in their last two games, but they say they can make the playoffs.
item.dc:creator: Dieter Kurtenbach, The Mercury News

item.title: Christian McCaffrey's season 'potentially' over after knee injury in 49ers' loss to Bills
item.description: San Francisco running back Christian McCaffrey suffered a knee injury that coach Kyle Shanahan described as “potentially” season-ending in the 49ers’ 35-10 loss to the Buffalo Bills on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Big Ten fines Michigan and Ohio State $100,000 each for postgame melee
item.description: ROSEMONT, Ill. (AP) — The Big Ten Conference announced it fined Michigan and Ohio State $100,000 each for violating the conference's sportsmanship policy for the on-field melee at the end of the Wolverines' win in Columbus on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ohio State, Michigan players involved in melee after Buckeyes loss, multiple people pepper sprayed
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (WCMH) — Michigan players planted their team flag at midfield of Ohio Stadium on Saturday afternoon after a 13-10 win over No. 2 Ohio State, leading to a brawl between players from both teams with police attempting to separate the sides and pepper spray being used.
item.dc:creator: Orri Benatar, KTLA 5

item.title: Aggies blow 17-point fourth quarter lead in loss to CSU
item.description: FORT COLLINS, Colo. (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah State football team could not hold a 30-13 fourth quarter lead in its season finale at Colorado State, and the Aggies end the season with a 42-37 loss to the Rams.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Los Angeles Dodgers extend contract of postseason hero
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers have agreed to a contract extension with one of the key pieces of the team's World Series title run. The Dodgers reached agreement Friday on a deal with Tommy Edman, ensuring that the do-it-all utility-man will remain with the team for the foreseeable future.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Spurgeon breaks 33-game scoreless streak with two goals, Minnesota beats Chicago 3-2
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Jared Spurgeon ended a 33-game scoring drought with two of Minnesota’s three second-period goals and the Wild beat the Chicago Blackhawks 3-2 on Friday. Marco Rossi also scored for Minnesota and Marc-Andre Fleury, who turned 40 on Thursday, made 20 saves to improve to 5-0-1.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Alabama A&M player Medrick Burnett Jr. dies following injury during game
item.description: Medrick Burnett Jr., a 20-year-old football player for Alabama A&M University, died Wednesday a month after sustaining a serious injury during a football game.
item.dc:creator: Drew Taylor, NewsNation

item.title: Taylor Swift spends Black Friday amid sea of red as boyfriend Travis Kelce's Chiefs face the Raiders
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Pop superstar Taylor Swift was spending Black Friday amid a sea of red to watch her boyfriend, Travis Kelce, and the rest of the Kansas City Chiefs as they played the Las Vegas Raiders in a chilly matinee at Arrowhead Stadium.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hall's fifth career hat trick leads Blackhawks over Stars 6-2
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Taylor Hall scored his fifth career hat trick, Connor Bedard, Teuvo Teravainen and Louis Crevier also scored, and the Chicago Blackhawks beat the Dallas Stars 6-2 on Wednesday night for their second win in three games. Hall’s game-opening goal 17 seconds in set the tone.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Federal appeals court upholds ruling to allow SJSU women's volleyball team member to play
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A federal appeals court upheld a ruling Tuesday that allows a San Jose State women’s volleyball team member to play in this week’s Mountain West Conference tournament after a legal complaint said she should be ineligible on grounds that she is transgender. The 10th U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4
